title: Cleisostoma fallax
taxonomy: Cleisostoma fallax is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Cleisostoma, which is a group of orchids native to Asia. The genus Cleisostoma is further divided into several species, with Cleisostoma fallax being one of them. The classification of Cleisostoma fallax is based on its unique characteristics and physical features, such as its flower shape and color. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Cleisostoma fallax is closely related to other orchid species.

intro: Cleisostoma fallax, also known as the fallax cleisostoma, is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Cleisostoma fallax include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into infusions, decoctions, or extracts, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, cough, and skin disorders.
